Title: Strigolactones act downstream of gibberellins to regulate fiber cell elongation and cell wall thickness in cotton (Gossypium hirsutum)

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes all-trans-β-carotene in the upstream step leading to carlactone and strigolactone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes cotton fiber cell elongation and cell wall thickness, improving fiber length and strength.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes epi-5DS accumulation, thereby increasing fiber elongation and cellulose-associated wall thickness; overexpression increases boll size and fiber length, knockout reduces plant height, increases branching, and shortens fibers.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; epi-5DS quantification; rescue with exogenous GR24; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling; phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in multiple tissues including fiber cells; elevated during fiber elongation stages (about 5–20 DPA).
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GR24; Tis108; GA3; paclobutrazol (PAC); in vitro ovule culture
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhD27 is the key SL biosynthetic gene highly expressed during cotton fiber elongation. 2. GhD27 overexpression increases epi-5DS and promotes longer fibers with thicker walls, whereas knockout lowers epi-5DS and causes shorter fibers. 3. GhD27 acts downstream of GA and upstream of SL-inducible TF modules controlling VLCFA and cellulose biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout caused more branches, reduced plant height, and smaller bolls; overexpression produced larger bolls. No major elongation-thickening penalty reported for overexpression.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong engineering target to improve fiber quality by increasing endogenous SL levels; can be combined with downstream cellulose/VLCFA regulators, but whole-plant architecture effects should be monitored.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The experimental functional allele emphasized in the Results is the D-subgenome copy; the abstract describes Gh_D11G1134, while accession list gives Gh_D07G2115, indicating locus inconsistency in the text.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Ketoacyl-CoA synthase; condensing enzyme of fatty acid elongase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes elongation in the VLCFA biosynthetic pathway and is implicated in conversion of C24:0 toward C26:0.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes VLCFA accumulation and fiber elongation; silencing reduces KCS5 expression, decreases C26:0 and shortens fibers.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR under GR24/Tis108 and GhD27 transgenics; VIGS; VLCFA profiling; yeast complementation in elo2 elo3 mutant
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in fibers by GR24 and in GhD27-OE; reduced by Tis108, GhD27 knockout, and silencing of upstream TFs.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhKCS5 is an SL-responsive VLCFA biosynthetic gene associated with cotton fiber elongation. 2. It is directly activated by GhBLH51 and GhNAC100-2. 3. Functional analyses indicate GhKCS5 mainly contributes to elongation from C24:0 to C26:0.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduces fiber length; no other tradeoffs reported.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for increasing fiber length through VLCFA enhancement, potentially stacked with SL/GA upstream regulators and cellulose-promoting modules.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Yeast complementation indicated broad VLCFA synthesis capacity with near-WT profile, consistent with a role in producing VLCFAs up to C26:0.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Ketoacyl-CoA synthase; condensing enzyme of fatty acid elongase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes elongation in the VLCFA biosynthetic pathway and is mainly responsible for synthesis of C22:0 from C20:0 fatty acids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes C22:0 accumulation and fiber elongation; silencing lowers C22:0 and shortens fibers.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR under GR24/Tis108 and GhD27 transgenics; VIGS; VLCFA profiling; yeast complementation in elo2 elo3 mutant
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in fibers by GR24 and in GhD27-OE; reduced by Tis108, GhD27 knockout, and silencing of upstream TFs.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhKCS23 is an SL-responsive VLCFA biosynthetic gene required for fiber elongation. 2. It is directly activated by GhGT2 and GhNAC100-2. 3. Functional assays indicate GhKCS23 mainly catalyzes conversion from C20:0 to C22:0.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduces fiber length; no other tradeoffs reported.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for metabolic engineering of cotton fiber elongation via selective enhancement of C22:0 biosynthesis, especially in combination with upstream SL-inducible TFs.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Yeast complementation restored viability but yielded high C22:0 with low C24:0 and no detectable C26:0, indicating substrate/product specialization distinct from GhKCS5.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes glucose from UDP-glucose into cellulose microfibrils at the plasma membrane.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Confers fiber cell wall thickness and mechanical strength.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cellulose accumulation and increased fiber cell wall thickness; expression is induced by SL signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; GR24/Tis108 treatments; transgenic GhD27 backgrounds; promoter binding by GhB9SHZ1 via Y1H/EMSA/LUC; cell wall staining and cellulose measurement
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GR24; Tis108; VIGS of upstream TFs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in fibers by GR24 and in GhD27-OE; reduced by Tis108, GhD27 knockout, GhGRF4 knockout, and GhB9SHZ1 silencing.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhCesA8 is an SL-responsive cellulose biosynthetic gene contributing to fiber wall thickening. 2. Its promoter is directly activated by GhB9SHZ1. 3. Reduced upstream SL signaling lowers GhCesA8 expression and cellulose accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for improving cotton fiber strength via secondary wall cellulose enhancement, particularly in coordinated engineering with elongation-promoting pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Treated as one of the key secondary wall CesA genes in cotton fiber.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes glucose from UDP-glucose into cellulose microfibrils at the plasma membrane.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Confers fiber cell wall thickness and mechanical strength.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cellulose accumulation and wall thickening; expression is activated by SL-inducible GhGT2.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; GR24/Tis108 treatments; promoter binding by GhGT2 via Y1H/EMSA/LUC; VIGS of GhGT2; cellulose and wall-thickness assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in fibers by GR24 and in GhD27-OE; reduced by Tis108, GhD27 knockout, GhGRF4 knockout, and GhGT2 silencing.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhCesA7-1 is an SL-responsive secondary wall cellulose synthase gene. 2. GhGT2 directly binds its promoter and activates its expression. 3. Loss of upstream signaling decreases cellulose deposition and fiber wall thickness.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve fiber strength by reinforcing secondary wall cellulose synthesis, especially alongside GhGT2 activation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes glucose from UDP-glucose into cellulose microfibrils at the plasma membrane.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Confers fiber cell wall thickness and mechanical strength.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cellulose accumulation and wall thickening; expression is activated by SL-inducible GhB9SHZ1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; GR24/Tis108 treatments; promoter binding by GhB9SHZ1 via Y1H/EMSA/LUC; VIGS of GhB9SHZ1; cellulose and wall-thickness assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in fibers by GR24 and in GhD27-OE; reduced by Tis108, GhD27 knockout, GhGRF4 knockout, and GhB9SHZ1 silencing.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhCesA7-2 is an SL-responsive secondary wall cellulose synthase gene. 2. GhB9SHZ1 directly activates its promoter. 3. Reduced upstream SL signaling lowers cellulose deposition and wall thickness.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for strengthening cotton fibers through enhanced cellulose biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Top-layer GA-inducible regulator linking GA signaling to SL biosynthesis; decisive bridge in GA-SL crosstalk.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: epi-5DS promotes fiber elongation and wall thickening through downstream VLCFA and cellulose biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates SL biosynthesis and thereby promotes fiber elongation and cell wall thickness; knockout reduces epi-5DS, downstream TF expression, cellulose, VLCFAs, fiber length, and wall thickness.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq candidate screen; Y1H; dual-LUC; EMSA; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; RT-qPCR; epi-5DS and metabolite measurements; phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in 25-DPA fibers, 0-DPA ovules, roots, and pistils; induced by GA3.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in fibers by GA3 and reduced by PAC.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhGRF4 is the only GA-inducible TF among tested candidates that directly binds the GhD27 promoter. 2. GhGRF4 activates GhD27 expression to increase SL accumulation. 3. GhGRF4 knockout suppresses the entire downstream SL-TF-KCS/CesA module and reduces fiber quality traits.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout causes severe reduction in fiber length and wall thickness; no overexpression tradeoff tested.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent upstream engineering target for coordinated improvement of fiber length and strength by activating endogenous GA-SL signaling, but broad GRF developmental effects should be considered.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Binds the first predicted GRF-binding site in the GhD27 promoter, not the second.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates GhCesA8 and GhCesA7-2 transcription.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Strigolactone/GR24; reduced by Tis108; dependent on GhD27 and GhGRF4
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Second-layer SL-inducible activator transmitting SL signals to cellulose synthase genes for wall thickening.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Increases fiber wall thickness and strength.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates cellulose accumulation and fiber cell wall thickness; silencing reduces CesA expression, cellulose content, and wall thickness.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; dual-LUC; EMSA; VIGS; RT-qPCR; cellulose measurement; microscopy
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by GR24 and repressed by Tis108; higher in GhD27-OE and lower in Ghd27-ko.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhB9SHZ1 is an SL-inducible TF that binds GhCesA8 and GhCesA7-2 promoters. 2. It activates cellulose synthase gene expression. 3. Silencing GhB9SHZ1 decreases cellulose accumulation and fiber wall thickness.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduces wall thickness; no broader tradeoffs reported.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream regulator for enhancing fiber strength through specific activation of cellulose biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct binding was mapped to the P2 fragment of GhCesA8 promoter and the P1 fragment of GhCesA7-2 promoter.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates GhCesA7-1 and GhKCS23 transcription.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Strigolactone/GR24; reduced by Tis108; dependent on GhD27 and GhGRF4
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Second-layer SL-inducible bifunctional regulator connecting SL signaling to both cellulose and VLCFA branches.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes fiber wall thickness and fiber elongation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates both cellulose and VLCFA biosynthetic genes; silencing reduces wall thickness, cellulose, KCS23 expression, C22:0 levels, and fiber length.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; dual-LUC; EMSA; VIGS; RT-qPCR; cellulose and VLCFA profiling; microscopy and fiber-length phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by GR24 and repressed by Tis108; higher in GhD27-OE and lower in Ghd27-ko.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhGT2 is an SL-inducible TF with dual control over cellulose and VLCFA pathways. 2. It directly activates GhCesA7-1 and GhKCS23. 3. Silencing GhGT2 decreases cell wall thickness and fiber elongation simultaneously.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing compromises both length and thickness; no additional tradeoffs reported.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A promising node for coordinated improvement of fiber length and strength because it links two metabolic outputs.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Binding mapped to the P5 fragment of GhCesA7-1 promoter and the P4 fragment of GhKCS23 promoter.

Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Strigolactone/GR24; reduced by Tis108; dependent on GhD27 and GhGRF4
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Second-layer SL-inducible activator controlling the C26:0-associated KCS5 branch for fiber elongation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates GhKCS5, increasing VLCFA synthesis required for fiber elongation; silencing reduces C26:0 and fiber length.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; dual-LUC; EMSA; VIGS; RT-qPCR; VLCFA profiling; fiber-length phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by GR24 and repressed by Tis108; higher in GhD27-OE and lower in Ghd27-ko.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhBLH51 is an SL-inducible TF that directly activates GhKCS5. 2. Silencing GhBLH51 lowers GhKCS5 expression and fiber length. 3. VLCFA profiling suggests this module is important for C24:0-to-C26:0 elongation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduces fiber length; no additional tradeoffs reported.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for improving fiber elongation through the specific KCS5/C26:0 branch of VLCFA metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Binding site mapped to the P1 fragment of the GhKCS5 promoter.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates GhKCS5 and GhKCS23 transcription.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Strigolactone/GR24; reduced by Tis108; dependent on GhD27 and GhGRF4
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Second-layer SL-inducible activator coordinating two VLCFA elongation steps for fiber elongation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates both GhKCS5 and GhKCS23; silencing lowers C22:0 and C26:0, increases C20:0 and C24:0, and shortens fibers.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; dual-LUC; EMSA; VIGS; RT-qPCR; VLCFA profiling; fiber-length phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by GR24 and repressed by Tis108; higher in GhD27-OE and lower in Ghd27-ko.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhNAC100-2 is an SL-inducible TF directly targeting GhKCS5 and GhKCS23. 2. It coordinates accumulation of elongation-associated VLCFAs. 3. Silencing GhNAC100-2 causes broad VLCFA defects and shorter fibers.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduces fiber length; no additional tradeoffs reported.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Good target for coordinated improvement of multiple VLCFA species that support fiber elongation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Binding mapped to the P1 fragments of both GhKCS5 and GhKCS23 promoters.
